name: eBay Inc.

profile.description: eBay Inc. operates a global e-commerce marketplace connecting buyers and sellers across 190 markets worldwide. The platform enables consumer-to-consumer and business-to-consumer sales through its Marketplace segment, specializing in non-new, collectible, and enthusiast inventory categories. Key verticals include motors, sneakers, luxury goods, trading cards, and refurbished electronics. The company also operates Depop (acquired 2021), a fashion resale platform. Founded in 1995, headquartered in San Jose, California, with a market cap of approximately $47.9 billion.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): eBay has executed meaningful product pivots in recent years, including the launch of eBay Live (live commerce/streaming platform), expansion into authenticated luxury goods and sneakers, and the strategic acquisition of Depop to capture the Gen Z resale market [1][2]. These represent genuine new revenue vectors beyond the legacy auction model, though none has yet redefined the company at the scale of the original marketplace. AI Deployment (1/2): eBay has deployed AI-powered seller tools that streamline listing creation, smarter search with personalized recommendations, and launched the "AI Activate" programme with OpenAI to provide free AI tools to small business sellers [3]. CEO Jamie Iannone has articulated a clear AI-first strategy, though quantified AI-driven revenue gains have not been publicly disclosed. Market Share (1/2): eBay maintains a meaningful but declining share of global e-commerce GMV. At $22.2B Q1 2026 GMV (up 18% YoY as-reported), the platform is growing but remains far behind Amazon in scale [1]. eBay is a category leader in collectibles, parts/accessories, and authenticated resale, but not in broader e-commerce. Product Pipeline (1/2): eBay has guided for 7-7.5% FX-neutral GMV growth for FY2026 and is investing in expanded eBay Live and AI-enhanced commerce experiences, but specific next-gen products with named launch dates are limited [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): eBay invests meaningfully in technology relative to its marketplace-model revenue base, and its innovation lab publishes regular AI research [3].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): eBay's mission is to provide "a global marketplace that connects buyers and sellers and enables economic opportunity for all." The mission is clear and specific to eBay's business — enabling economic empowerment through commerce — but it lacks the transformative depth of a true Just Cause. It describes what eBay does more than the future it envisions. Community Impact (2/2): The eBay Foundation is a standout. In 2025, 73% of eBay's workforce participated in giving or volunteering — more than 3x the industry average of 21%. Over 2,300 employees volunteered 60,000+ hours. Employee engagement in Foundation programs grew 85% YoY to 8,521 unique participants [4]. This is specific, measurable, and distinctive community investment. Employee Retention (1/2): eBay maintained 95% Glassdoor positivity in 2025 and reduced employee burnout by 10 points. U.S. benefits satisfaction rose from 82% to 86%, with five consecutive years of no medical premium increases [5]. While specific turnover or tenure data is not publicly reported, these metrics suggest above-average employee satisfaction. Insider Buying (1/2): Net insider selling dominates. CEO Jamie Iannone sold approximately $8.4 million in shares between April-May 2026 under a Rule 10b5-1 plan, while acquiring only 302 shares via ESPP [6]. He retains 509,371 shares ($56M), signaling continued alignment, but active net selling reduces the score.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. eBay delivered $11.60B TTM revenue with Q1 2026 revenue of $3.09B up 19% YoY as-reported [1]. ROE of 42.88% reflects exceptional capital efficiency. Profit margin of 17.61% and operating margin of 21.83% demonstrate strong marketplace economics with 71.79% gross margins. D/E of 163% is elevated due to aggressive share buybacks (5.41% buyback yield) reducing equity base, but the debt is manageable with $47.9B market cap and strong FCF generation. Forward P/E of 17.51 is attractive relative to growth. PEG of 1.68 reflects reasonable valuation for mid-single-digit organic growth. Main weakness is the 5-year revenue CAGR of approximately 1.6%, reflecting the post-pandemic normalization from 2021's $10.42B peak through the 2022-2024 trough before the 2025 recovery to $11.10B [1]. Liquidity is strong at $107.87 x 4.14M avg daily volume = $446.8M daily liquidity.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (4/9): eBay faces meaningful AI disruption risk. Agentic commerce — where AI shopping agents autonomously search, compare, and purchase across platforms — threatens eBay's core marketplace intermediation model. Retail Dive and Payments Dive have reported that large language models from Perplexity AI, OpenAI, and Google moving into agentic commerce are "potentially disruptive" to eBay's model [7]. Consumer shopping agents are expected to move from product research to fully autonomous transactions, which could bypass eBay's platform entirely. The BCG Consumer AI Disruption Index identifies e-commerce marketplaces as a moderately exposed category [8]. However, eBay's focus on non-new, unique, and collectible inventory creates some defensibility — AI agents need structured catalogs to function, and eBay's one-of-a-kind inventory is harder to intermediate than commodity goods. The physical nature of the goods themselves provides a floor of protection. AI Opportunity (1/1): eBay is actively deploying AI across its platform — AI-powered listing tools, personalized search, the AI Activate programme with OpenAI, and AI-driven authentication and fraud detection [3]. While specific AI revenue figures are not disclosed, AI is integrated into the core product experience and measurably improving seller productivity.

infinite_game.summary: eBay under Jamie Iannone plays a mixed game — elements of infinite thinking in its recommitment to enthusiast categories and long-term marketplace health, but the company's history of strategic pivots every few years (PayPal spinoff, StubHub sale, managed payments, focused categories, AI-first) suggests a company still searching for its enduring identity rather than executing on a validated Just Cause.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): eBay's purpose of enabling economic opportunity through connected commerce is genuine and has shaped strategic decisions — the focus on small sellers, the eBay Foundation's entrepreneurship programs, and the circular economy positioning all trace back to this purpose. However, the mission hasn't been tested through the kind of multi-decade consistency that earns a 3. eBay has reinvented its strategic narrative multiple times (auction site, PayPal parent, managed payments, focused verticals, AI-first), suggesting the purpose statement follows the strategy rather than driving it [4].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): eBay shows adequate organizational trust. The 95% Glassdoor positivity, five consecutive years of no medical premium increases, and strong Foundation participation (73% workforce engagement) suggest employees feel valued [5]. Iannone's emphasis on "reverse mentoring" — senior leaders learning from junior AI-native employees — signals humility. However, eBay has gone through significant workforce reductions in recent years, and the company lacks standout data on voluntary turnover or exceptional cultural practices that would merit a higher score.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): eBay acknowledges its competitors' strengths and uses them to refine its own positioning. Rather than trying to out-Amazon Amazon on commodity goods, Iannone has deliberately positioned eBay as the destination for enthusiast, collectible, and non-new inventory — a strategic choice that implicitly acknowledges Amazon's dominance in commodity e-commerce and uses it as motivation to differentiate [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): eBay has shown incremental flexibility — the PayPal spinoff (2015), StubHub sale (2020), managed payments transition, Depop acquisition, and pivot to focused verticals all represent meaningful strategic evolution [2]. However, none of these constitutes a truly transformative reinvention driven by a Just Cause. The company has adapted to survive rather than made a bold, purpose-driven bet that redefined the business.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Iannone's decision to narrow eBay's focus to enthusiast categories — walking away from broad commodity e-commerce where Amazon dominates — represents a courageous strategic choice that sacrificed addressable market for defensibility. The aggressive $5.4B+ annual buyback program and consistent dividend growth show capital allocation discipline. However, these decisions were arguably pragmatic survival moves rather than costly long-term bets made in service of a higher purpose [1].

investment_thesis: eBay delivered strong Q1 2026 results with revenue of $3.09B (up 19% as-reported, 17% FX-neutral) and GMV of $22.2B (up 18%), demonstrating that the focused category strategy under CEO Jamie Iannone is generating real momentum [1]. Non-GAAP EPS of $1.66 was up 21% YoY, with operating margins expanding to 29.4% non-GAAP. The marketplace model generates exceptional economics — 71.8% gross margins, 42.9% ROE, and sufficient free cash flow to fund a 5.4% buyback yield plus a growing 1.15% dividend. At 17.5x forward earnings, eBay trades at a meaningful discount to e-commerce peers despite accelerating growth. The AI Activate programme with OpenAI, eBay Live streaming commerce, and authenticated luxury verticals provide multiple vectors for continued GMV expansion. The Depop acquisition strengthens eBay's position in the high-growth Gen Z resale market.
thesis_continued: The primary risk is that eBay's growth acceleration proves temporary rather than structural. Q2 2026 guidance of 8-10% FX-neutral revenue growth represents a significant deceleration from Q1's 17%, and the full-year GMV guidance of only 7-7.5% FX-neutral growth suggests management itself expects moderation [1]. The 5-year revenue CAGR of approximately 1.6% reveals that eBay has essentially been flat over a multi-year period, with the current upturn following a post-pandemic contraction. More fundamentally, agentic AI commerce poses a structural threat to marketplace intermediation — if AI shopping agents can search, compare, and transact across platforms autonomously, eBay's value as a discovery and transaction layer could erode [7]. The debt-to-equity ratio of 163% reflects aggressive financial engineering through buybacks, and the Inspire Impact Score of -100 (MISALIGNED) may concern faith-based investors. CEO insider selling of $8.4M in recent months, while conducted under a pre-arranged trading plan, does not inspire confidence in near-term upside [6].
key_catalysts: Continued GMV acceleration driven by eBay Live streaming commerce expansion, AI-powered listing tools increasing seller productivity, and authenticated luxury/sneaker vertical growth
AI Activate programme with OpenAI could meaningfully improve small seller conversion rates and listing quality, driving platform-wide GMV growth beyond the current 7-7.5% FX-neutral guidance
Depop integration and Gen Z resale market capture, combined with circular economy positioning, provides secular growth tailwind as younger consumers shift toward secondhand commerce
key_risks: Agentic AI commerce from Perplexity, OpenAI, and Google threatens marketplace intermediation by enabling autonomous cross-platform shopping that could bypass eBay's discovery and transaction layer entirely
Q1 2026 growth of 17-19% may prove unsustainable as Q2 guidance moderates to 8-10%, and the 5-year revenue CAGR of 1.6% suggests structural low-single-digit organic growth is the base case

Debt-to-equity of 163% driven by aggressive buybacks creates balance sheet risk if marketplace economics deteriorate, while $3.35B net debt limits financial flexibility for acquisitions or defensive investment
